title: Substack vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Constant Contact. Explore pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Substack vs Constant Contact: A Complete Comparison
intro: Substack and Constant Contact serve different segments of the email marketing landscape. Substack caters to individual creators and writers seeking direct monetization, while Constant Contact targets small businesses and nonprofits needing comprehensive marketing tools. Understanding their distinct strengths will help you choose the right platform for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Yes, unlimited subscribers with no cost

comparison_table.tool_b: Email marketing, SMS, social ads, and e-commerce

comparison_table.tool_a: Writers and creators monetizing directly from audiences
comparison_table.tool_b: Small businesses, nonprofits, and local services

comparison_table.tool_a: Podcast hosting, notes feed, built-in audience discovery
comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS marketing, social media ads, e-commerce

choose_a_if: You want to monetize content directly through paid subscriptions with no upfront costs
You're a writer or creator focused on building an audience around your work
You prefer a simple platform dedicated to newsletters without additional marketing tools
choose_b_if: You need comprehensive marketing features including SMS, social ads, and e-commerce integration
You run a small business or nonprofit and want an affordable all-in-one solution
You require event management, list-building tools, and professional email templates

faq.q: Is Substack better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Constant Contact is superior for businesses managing customer relationships and multi-channel marketing. Your choice depends on whether you prioritize creator monetization or comprehensive business marketing features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for getting started, offering a completely free tier with unlimited subscribers. Constant Contact requires payment starting at $12/month. However, Substack takes 10% of paid subscription revenue, which may cost more as you scale—making total cost-of-ownership situation-dependent.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your email list from Constant Contact and import it into Substack, though the platforms serve different purposes. Substack is designed for creator monetization rather than traditional business marketing, so consider whether its features align with your needs before switching.
